Art title 1: Station 3 Jesus is Condemned by the Sanheidrin
Artist Statement 1: With this mandala I wanted to capture the vicious atmosphere Jesuswas placed under. Large canine teeth and bloody viper mouths aresuggested to show a more tangible expression of the hearts of thoseaccusing Jesus. Everything is symmetric radially except for Jesus'face at the middle.
Story Title again: ANIMATION: Station 3 Jesus is Condemned by the Sanheidrin
excerpt:
(See video below) When day came the council of elders of the people met, both chief priests and scribes, and they brought him before their Sanhedrin. They said, "If you are the Messiah, tell us," but he replied to them, "If I tell you, you will not believe, and if I question, you will not respond. But from this time on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God." They all asked, "Are you then the Son of God?" He replied to them, "You say that I am." Then they said, "What further need have we for testimony? We have heard it from his own mouth."

(Luke 22: 66--71, NAB)


Lord, grant us your sense of righteousness, that we may never cease to work 
to bring about the justice of the kingdom that you promised.

First we look at the crowd of important people throwing a mountain of blame on him. Ask yourself this question: Am I being ganged up on or blamed in any way for something, in my family, at work, or among my friends? Do I sometimes see them disrespecting me by rolling their eyes to each other after something I do? Where do you feel misunderstood and blamed?

Also, now ask yourself, "do I harbor bits of hate as I blame other people, perhaps those above me dictating my future?

Now we look at some of the phrases going back and forth. They are about Jesus's identity. "Who are you?!" "You wouldn't believe me." "Tell us!" "I am the son of God." "Liar!" They might have also said or meant, "You actually think you are a child of God? You're crazy." What things in your life make you doubt that you are a beloved child of God?

Jesus was tempted to doubt too. 
Now I just want to note the jagged lines, images of dread, and vicious wild animals all around Jesus. Have you felt unsafe with the people around you? Are you stuck in a caustic environment with people you feel are untamed and could snap at any moment? Jesus felt this too. 

Reflect on his response and yours. What allowed him to respond the way he did? 
And lastly, we look at Jesus's suffering face, one eye swollen shut already. We see everything in this mandala pointing at him, scornfully blaming him, though it is undeserved. For a few moments, think about the situations that this image has brought up for you. Jesus probably felt something very similar. 

Now look at the three circles behind Jesus's head, and remember the triune God, Who is in charge of all and can be trusted with our story. And He can also forgive all of us for the ways we accuse others. God is for us in all of our suffering and longs to rescue us from our sin.

It feels very dark right now, my friends, and it will get darker, but the light is coming.

Amen.
